items.title: Atlético laat Barcelona terugdenken aan die ene nederlaag tegen Bayern
items.summary: Atlético Madrid heeft Barcelona naar het kastje van de muur gespeeld in de eerste helft van het heenduel van de halve finale in de Copa del Rey. Voor het eerst sinds de beschamende nederlaag in de Champions League tegen Bayern München (2-8) kregen de Catalanen weer eens vier goals om de oren voor de rust.

items.title: Atlético rip Barça apart in Copa semi 1st leg rout
items.summary: Atlético Madrid shocked Barcelona with four goals in the first half to win the first leg of their Copa del Rey semifinal 4-0 at the Metropolitano stadium.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to Man Utd job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Sterling joins Feyenoord after Chelsea exit
items.summary: Raheem Sterling has agreed deal to join Feyenoord until end of season, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Arsenal drop title race points in Brentford draw
items.summary: Arsenal extended their lead at the Premier League to four points after they were held at Brentford on Thursday.

items.title: Koploper Arsenal ziet Manchester City naderen in de Premier League
items.summary: De loting van de Nations League met het Nederlands elftal, de afstraffing van Barcelona in de Copa del Rey tegen Atlético Madrid en het puntenverlies van Arsenal in de Premier League. Alles kwam voorbij in de VI Live.

items.title: Keane Lewis-Potter header earns Brentford point to deny leaders Arsenal
items.summary: This was billed as a test of Arsenal’s mettle after Manchester City had picked themselves off the canvas against Liverpool at Anfield to reignite the title race. But having seen their lead at the top whittled down from nine points to just three since the weekend, Mikel Arteta’s side showed their fallibility as they were held by a dogged Brentford side who will feel that they should have even claimed victory. Just when they needed to put in a statement performance, Arsenal were edgy throughout and struggled to create anything of note until Noni Madueke opened the scoring. Keane Lewis-Potter’s equaliser that came from a long throw had been coming as Keith Andrews’ side showed exactly why they are enjoying such a successful season since he replaced Thomas Frank last summer. Continue reading...

items.title: Barça komt horrorhelft niet te boven: Atlético met één been in bekerfinale
items.summary: Barcelona is donderdagavond flink tegen de lamp gelopen in het heenduel van de halve finale van de Copa del Rey. Atlético Madrid besliste de wedstrijd al in de eerste helft door vier goals te maken.

items.title: Super League newcomers York claim shock win over Hull KR in season opener
items.summary: York Knights pulled off the most magical of victories as they got their first season in the Betfred Super League up and running with a 19-18 victory over defending champions Hull KR in the opening match of the campaign.

items.title: Terland and Malard set Manchester United on course for last eight in win over Atlético
items.summary: Manchester United secured an important 3-0 lead in the first leg of their Women’s Champions League playoff against Atlético Madrid. Goals from Elisabeth Terland and Melvine Malard bookended the first half before Julia Zigiotti Olme added a third late on as Marc Skinner’s side produced a professional performance away from home. These two were familiar opponents having met in Madrid back in October in the league phase. United came out with a 1-0 win on that occasion in an eventful encounter in which both sides were reduced to 10 players. Continue reading...

items.title: Price flexes his muscles to win Premier League night in Antwerp
items.summary: Gerwyn Price flexed his muscles to defeat Michael van Gerwen 6-3 and seal his first Premier League nightly win of the season in Antwerp on Thursday, with Luke Littler and Luke Humphries both sitting outside the top four.

items.title: Arsenal drop more title-race points with draw at Brentford
items.summary: Arsenal missed the chance to re-establish their six-point lead at the top of the Premier League as an impressive Brentford side held them to a 1-1 draw.

items.title: Kampioenbibbers bij Arsenal: puntenverlies bij Brentford door assist Van den Berg
items.summary: Arsenal heeft donderdagavond punten verspeeld in de Premier League. De koploper kwam niet verder dan een gelijkspel op bezoek bij Brentford. Omdat Manchester City eerder in de speelronde wel won, loopt de achtervolger twee punten in.

items.title: Oosting ziet bekerfinale door vingers glippen: Anderlecht declasseert Antwerp
items.summary: Anderlecht heeft de finale van de Croky Cup bereikt. Het trainerloze Paars-Wit maakte en statement in de return tegen het Antwerp FC van Joseph Oosting: het werd liefst 0-4 op De Bosuil.

items.title: Tuchel extended England stay for 'amazing players'
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel says he extended his contract as England manager through to Euro 2028 because of the "amazing group of players" at his disposal.

items.title: 'We're not quick enough' - Norris says McLaren are behind Ferrari, Red Bull
items.summary: Lando Norris believes world champions McLaren currently trail Ferrari and Red Bull on pace with four days of pre-season testing to go.

items.title: Fury comeback fight to be held at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium
items.summary: Tyson Fury’s comeback fight against Arslanbek Makhmudov will take place at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium when he returns to boxing on April 11, according to promoter Turki Alalshikh.

items.title: Football must reject Sir Jim Ratcliffe’s cynical, self-serving electioneering | Barney Ronay
items.summary: Tax exile has already proven himself a terrible club owner; now his ill-informed diatribe about immigration has poured fuel on wider flames Well I, for one, am shocked. Shocked to learn that a tax-exiled English expat who made his billions squeezing chemical plants doesn’t have liberal, let alone accurate, views on immigration. Or at least, in public anyway. It seems highly likely Sir Jim Ratcliffe knew what he was doing in the course of his now semi-recanted Sky News interview. And it is above all vital that at least one part of his empire of influence – football, sport, Manchester United – rejects it, as the club have done to some extent in their statement. Continue reading...

items.title: NI relish 'competitive' Nations League draw - O'Neill
items.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill says "there are no easy games" in the Nations League after being drawn with Hungary, Ukraine and Georgia.
